title: Sutton SignWriting Language and Literacy Research Overview
subtitle: Developmental pathways, literacy layering, reading questions, and signed-language research
summary: Language pathways, literacy layering, reading questions, and study design define the research agenda opened by signed-language writing.

slides.title: What This Series Studies
slides.summary: Signed-language writing opens a research program about language pathways, literacy, reading, and study design.

image_prompts.concrete: Research overview slide showing language pathways, literacy pathways, reading signed text, study design, corpora, and data infrastructure
image_prompts.abstract: A research program opening from durable signed text into study pathways

bullets: Questions opened by durable signed writing
slides.speech: This is the research-facing series. It asks what kinds of language, literacy, reading, and study-design questions become possible when signed languages have durable written text.
slides.notes: Frame as research agenda, not proof of outcomes.

slides.title: Research Posture
slides.summary: The series distinguishes conceptual possibility, documented use, research design, and outcome evidence.

image_prompts.concrete: Four-layer research slide labeled conceptual possibility, documented use, study design, and outcome evidence
image_prompts.abstract: Research layers separated by clear boundaries

bullets: Use documented practice as research material
Treat outcomes as open empirical questions
Keep participant and stakeholder review visible
slides.speech: The research posture is careful. Documented use matters, but it is not the same as outcome evidence. Conceptual possibility matters, but it is not the same as a completed study. The series keeps those layers separate so future research has a cleaner starting point.

slides.title: Reading Order
slides.summary: The series moves from language pathways into research-program design and reading questions.

image_prompts.concrete: Research reading path slide moving through Four Language Pathways, Research Program for Literacy Without Sound, and Reading Signed Text
image_prompts.abstract: A research path moving from pathways to program design to reading

bullets: Research Program for Literacy Without Sound
slides.speech: The clearest path is to begin with The Four Language Pathways, then read the research program entry, then move into Reading Signed Text. The overview explains how those pieces fit together.
slides.notes: Keep this as a practical map.

slides.title: Claim Boundaries
slides.summary: The series defines a research space without treating documented use, conceptual possibility, or infrastructure readiness as completed outcome evidence.

image_prompts.concrete: Research boundary slide separating conceptual model, documented use, study design, infrastructure readiness, and outcome evidence for signed-language literacy
image_prompts.abstract: A research map with clear boundaries between possible, documented, designed, and proven

bullets: Conceptual possibility is not outcome evidence
slides.speech: The series is strongest when the evidence layers stay separate. Conceptual possibility is not outcome evidence. Documented use is not a completed study. Infrastructure makes better studies possible, but it does not replace the studies. The goal is to define a research space clearly enough that future work can test it.
slides.notes: This slide should make the overview sound research-facing and disciplined. It protects the series from being read as a finished claims package.
